title: Catalyst part, and ventilation filter, ventilation plug, and lead-acid battery including the same
abstract: Provided is a catalyst part for a lead-acid battery, the catalyst part being capable of reducing gas release from an electrolyte solution and a decrease in electrolyte solution due to the leakage, thus providing a lead-acid battery having a long life. Also provided are a ventilation filter, a ventilation plug, and a lead-acid battery each including the catalyst part. A catalyst part for a lead-acid battery, including a catalyst layer including a catalyst to accelerate a reaction for generating water or water vapor from oxygen and hydrogen, and an arrangement through which at least part of the water or water vapor is condensed and/or flowed back to the inside of the battery.
claims:
Claims [Claim 1] A catalyst part for a lead-acid battery, comprising: a catalyst layer comprising a catalyst to accelerate a reaction for generating water or water vapor from oxygen and hydrogen; and an arrangement through which at least part of the water or water vapor is condensed and/or flowed back to the inside of the battery, wherein the catalyst part is gas permeable as a whole . [Claim 2] The catalyst part according to Claim 1, further comprising a member capable of promoting the condensation of at least part of the water or water vapor, on the catalyst layer. [Claim 3] The catalyst part according to Claim 2, wherein the condensation-promoting member is a hydrophilic porous member . [Claim 4] The catalyst part according to Claim 3, wherein the hydrophilic porous member is made of expanded polytetrafluoroethylene subjected to a hydrophilization treatment . [Claim 5] The catalyst part according to Claim 4, wherein a metal oxide gel is used for the hydrophilization treatment. [Claim 6] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 5, further comprising a wall member extending from the adjacence to the periphery of the catalyst layer toward the inside of the lead-acid battery, wherein at least part of the water or water vapor generated by the catalysis is able to be condensed on the wall member. [Claim 7] 

 The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 6, wherein at least part of the arrangement is arranged on the periphery of the catalyst layer. [Claim 8] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 7, wherein the catalyst layer comprises a hydrophobic porous member. [Claim 9] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 8, wherein a catalyst support supporting the catalyst is arranged in the cavities or on the surface of the pores of the catalyst layer. [Claim 10] The catalyst part according to Claim 9, wherein the catalyst layer is made by mixing the catalyst support with polytetrafluoroethylene and then making porous the mixture thereof. [Claim 11] The catalyst part according to Claim 9 or 10, wherein the catalyst layer is made by mixing the catalyst support with polytetrafluoroethylene and then making porous the mixture thereof through expansion. [Claim 12] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 11, further comprising an inner hydrophobic porous member being capable of repelling moisture, allowing gas to pass, and trapping particulate matter, wherein the inner hydrophobic porous member is located closer to the inside of the lead-acid battery than the catalyst layer is. [Claim 13] The catalyst part according to Claim 12, further comprising a substance capable of absorbing a catalytic poison inside the inner hydrophobic porous member. [Claim 14] The catalyst part according to Claim 13, wherein the substance capable of absorbing a catalytic poison is 

arranged in the cavities or on the surface of the pores of a hydrophobic porous member. [Claim 15] The catalyst part according to Claim 14, wherein the hydrophobic porous member is made by mixing the substance capable of absorbing a catalytic poison with polytetrafluoroethylene and then making porous the mixture thereof. [Claim 16] The. catalyst part according to Claim 14 or 15, wherein the hydrophobic porous member is made by mixing the substance capable of absorbing a catalytic poison with polytetrafluoroethylene and then making porous the mixture thereof through expansion. [Claim 17] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 12 to 16, wherein at least part of the arrangement is arranged on the periphery of the inner hydrophobic porous member . [Claim 18] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 17, wherein at least part of the arrangement further comprises a spacer defining a space above the catalyst layer . [Claim 19] The catalyst part according to Claim 18, wherein the spacer is a woven fabric, a nonwoven fabric, a knitted fabric, a porous membrane, a flame arrester (an explosion-proof filter) , a column spacer, or an O-ring. [Claim 20] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 19, further comprising an outer hydrophobic porous member being capable of repelling moisture, allowing gas to pass, wherein the outer hydrophobic porous member is located closer to the outside of the lead-acid battery than the catalyst layer is. [Claim 21] 

 The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 20, further comprising a diffusion channel member capable of suppressing water vapor diffusion and allowing gas to pass, wherein the diffusion channel member is located closer to the outside of the lead-acid battery than the catalyst layer is. [Claim 22] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 21, wherein at least part of the water or water vapor generated by catalysis is repelled by or passes through the inner hydrophobic porous member, the catalyst layer, and/or the outer hydrophobic porous member, and is caused to flow back to the inside of the lead-acid battery. [Claim 23] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 22, further comprising a baffle located closer to the inside of the lead-acid battery than the catalyst layer is, wherein the baffle prevents a gas flow from an electrolyte solution inside the lead-acid battery from reaching the catalyst layer. [Claim 24] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 23, further comprising a baffle located closer to the inside of the lead-acid battery than the catalyst layer is, wherein the baffle leans toward the inside of the lead-acid battery and facilitates sliding down of liquid drops to the inside of the lead-acid battery. [Claim 25] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 24, further comprising an aerosol separator located closer to the inside of the lead-acid battery than the catalyst layer is, the aerosol separator having a tube having a first open end located closer to the electrolyte solution inside the lead-acid battery and a second open end located farther away from the electrolyte solution and communicating with the catalyst layer, wherein a gas flow from the electrolyte solution is detoured radially 

 outward in the tube and turned toward the catalyst layer, thereby centrifugally separating liquid drop components contained in the gas flow. [Claim 26] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 25, wherein at least one of the inner hydrophobic porous member, the catalyst layer, and the outer hydrophobic porous member is made of porous polytetrafluoroethylene . [Claim 27] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 21 to 26, wherein the diffusion channel member has a structure to allow a gas to leak when the pressure inside the lead-acid battery exceeds a prescribed value, thereby reducing the pressure inside the lead-acid battery. [Claim 28] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 20 to 27, further comprising a double-lid structure to flow back water repelled by the outer hydrophobic porous member to the inside of the lead-acid battery. [Claim 29] The catalyst part according to Claim 28, wherein at least part of the double-lid structure is constructed in the arrangement. [Claim 30] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 29, wherein when the lead-acid battery comprises cells, each of the cells is provided with the catalyst part. [Claim 31] The catalyst part according to any one of Claims 9 to 30, wherein the support contains a carbon material. [Claim 32] A ventilation filter for a lead-acid battery, comprising the catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 31. [Claim 33] 

 A ventilation plug for a lead-acid battery, comprising the catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 31. [Claim 34] A lead-acid battery comprising the catalyst part according to any one of Claims 1 to 31.
